title: abronia elliptica
intro: Abronia elliptica, also known as desert marigold or apricot mallow, is a flowering plant species in the genus Abronia. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, and has been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems. Abronia elliptica can be prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil, and has been used to promote overall health and well-being, including reducing inflammation and improving digestion.
